action: search for existing service first, add method to service layer
check command registration order in extension.ts, ensure dependencies initialized first

trigger: test uses Date.now() or real time
action: use DeterministicTime from test utilities for consistent behavior
use toError() from @snapback-oss/sdk/utils/errorHelpers.ts
use withRetry() from @snapback-oss/sdk/utils/retry.ts, don't create new

trigger: vitest config OR test configuration OR new package tests
action: use @snapback/vitest-config with nodeConfig/jsdomConfig/vscodeConfig presets - see Test Infrastructure Rules in ROUTER.md
Check all tests for constructor signature changes, update mock contexts, verify group keys match type definitions (hyphenated not camelCase)

trigger: Adding ExtensionContext parameter to VS Code provider
action: Add mockContext with globalState.get/update to ALL test instantiations, including static register() methods
Tests navigate via item.data.type not item.label; create helper functions for tree traversal; update all label/description assertions
Organize commits by type: 1) feat: implementation, 2) test: test updates, 3) chore: infrastructure, 4) docs: documentation
use fire-and-forget pattern with Promise.resolve()
trace execution flow to identify timing overhead and unaccounted time

trigger: duplicate service instances across activation phases
action: search for 'new ServiceName(' in extension.ts and phase files to detect duplicates
MUST call codebase_get_context BEFORE implementation, codebase_check_patterns BEFORE committing, and codebase_record_learning AFTER completing
Update MCP config to use server name 'codebase' instead of 'snapback-internal'. Tools are now codebase:get_context, codebase:check_patterns, etc.

Use sql.js instead of better-sqlite3 when native compilation is problematic (e.g., ai_dev_utils outside workspace). sql.js uses different API: db.prepare().step().getAsObject() instead of db.prepare().get(). Always save db with saveDb() after writes.

Implement validation pipeline with 7 parallel layers (syntax, types, tests, architecture, security, dependencies, performance). Use confidence thresholds: 0 critical → 95%, 0 issues → 95%, ≤2 issues → 70%, ≤5 issues → 50%, else → 20%. Map to auto_merge/quick_review/full_review.

Learning engine tracks interactions with log_interaction, records feedback with record_feedback, builds golden dataset from high-confidence correct examples (5+ per query type = context promotion). Query classification uses keyword matching for: authentication, testing, api, database, ui, vscode, mcp, performance, architecture.
Found: chokidar ALREADY IN USE (4+ locations), withRetry exists in @snapback-oss/sdk, bottleneck in tests, custom DBSCAN with tests. Update lib_implementation.md to reflect actual state.

ai_dev_utils/mcp uses --ignore-workspace flag for pnpm install since it's outside main workspace. Libraries integrated: atomically (safe writes), p-retry (resilient API), bottleneck (rate limiting), chokidar (pattern file watching).
Use --ignore-workspace flag since ai_dev_utils is external to main monorepo workspace. Command: pnpm add <packages> --ignore-workspace

Integrated 4 libraries into ai_dev_utils/mcp: atomically (safe writes), p-retry (resilient API), bottleneck (rate limiting), chokidar (pattern watching). Used --ignore-workspace flag for pnpm. ml-dbscan deferred - custom impl is well-tested.

Extract ai_dev_utils/mcp intelligence into @snapback/intelligence package. Same algorithms, different configs. Internal use: rootDir='ai_dev_utils', Product use: rootDir=workspace. Key modules: ContextEngine (semantic retrieval), ValidationPipeline (7 layers), LearningEngine (feedback loop), ViolationTracker (auto-promotion).
Always add defensive null/undefined checks at the start of validation layer methods. Pattern: if (!code || !filePath) { return { issues }; }
ai_dev_utils/mcp must be added to pnpm-workspace.yaml for workspace:* dependencies to resolve. Add '- ai_dev_utils/mcp' to packages list.
Use @snapback/intelligence package for validation, learning, and context retrieval. Import from main facade or subpaths: @snapback/intelligence/validation, /learning, /context, /storage.
Externalize @huggingface/transformers, sql.js, and onnxruntime-* in tsup.config.ts when using dynamic imports for optional dependencies. They cause build failures if bundled.

EventBus consolidated from @snapback/events to @snapback/contracts. Key steps: 1) Create eventBus.emitter.ts with generic onRequest/request methods 2) Export from contracts/index.ts 3) Update all imports 4) Remove package.json dependencies 5) Delete old package directory

Phase 2 completed: policy-engine → intelligence migration. Key steps: 1) Create policy/ directory in intelligence 2) Copy PolicyEngine.ts, SarifFormatter.ts, detectors/ 3) Create policy/index.ts with all exports 4) Add subpath export to package.json 5) Update tsup.config.ts with policy entry 6) Update consumers to @snapback/intelligence/policy 7) Remove stale tsconfig references 8) Delete old package

trigger: package consolidation duplicate code workspace deduplication
action: When consolidating duplicates: 1) Build canonical package FIRST 2) Update imports in dependent files 3) Delete duplicates 4) Update tests with minimal config (LearningEngine needs rootDir + learningsDir, ValidationPipeline needs no args) 5) Verify all tests pass before marking complete. Common pattern: MCP server duplicated SemanticRetriever (444 lines), LearningEngine (390 lines), ValidationPipeline (603 lines) - all migrated to @snapback/intelligence with workspace:* protocol.

trigger: mcp dual-use internal external customer architecture intelligence
action: Dual-use MCP architecture: Internal server (ai_dev_utils/mcp) uses name 'codebase' with rootDir='ai_dev_utils' for SnapBack dev team. External server (@snapback/intelligence) uses name 'snapback' with rootDir=customerWorkspace for platform customers. Same intelligence algorithms (ValidationPipeline, LearningEngine, SemanticRetriever), different data sources. ROUTER.md clarifies internal-only scope. packages/intelligence/README.md documents customer-facing MCP setup with mcp_snapback_* tools.

When removing duplicate packages: 1) Grep for actual imports (not just comments) 2) Check package.json dependencies 3) Verify functionality exists elsewhere before removal 4) Common pattern: @snapback/analytics duplicated @snapback/infrastructure/metrics functionality

trigger: package consolidation duplicate zero consumers fast removal
action: Fast package removal pattern for duplicates: 1) Grep imports (not comments) 2) Check package.json deps 3) If zero usage, delete immediately 4) Run pnpm install 5) Verify build. No migration code needed. Both @snapback/analytics and @snapback/mail were duplicates with zero actual consumers - completed in 15 min vs estimated 3-4 hours.

trigger: mcp path resolution cwd dirname bug
action: prompt-cache.ts had wrong path resolution: used process.cwd() instead of fileURLToPath(import.meta.url). When MCP server is launched with absolute path via .mcp.json, process.cwd() is project root, not script directory. Always use __dirname pattern from server.ts for relative paths in MCP tools.

@huggingface/transformers requires onnxruntime-common and onnxruntime-node as transitive dependencies. When SemanticRetriever fails with 'Cannot find package onnxruntime-common', install both: pnpm add -w onnxruntime-common onnxruntime-node. Also must run indexContextFiles() to populate embeddings.db before semantic retrieval works.

trigger: dual-use MCP, customer MCP, internal vs external MCP
action: For dual-use MCP architecture: 1) Same intelligence package (@snapback/intelligence) for both, 2) Internal uses rootDir='ai_dev_utils' with mcp_codebase_* prefix, 3) External uses rootDir=customerWorkspace with snapback.* prefix, 4) Internal has ROUTER.md access, external doesn't. See ROUTER.md lines 306-316 and unified_context_system.md 'Dual-Use Architecture' section.

trigger: vague assertion fix pattern toBeDefined VAGUE_ASSERTION
action: Replace toBeDefined() with specific type checks like expect(issue?.type).toBe('EXPECTED_TYPE'). Keep toBeUndefined() for asserting absence of violations - this is valid specific assertion. Fixed 20 vague assertions across 3 test files in ai_dev_utils/mcp.
Add describe blocks for Edge Cases and Error Handling with at least 3 tests each: boundary conditions, concurrent operations, malformed input, corrupted data, db failures

trigger: vitest failed to resolve package ESM, No loader for .md files
action: When fixing vitest workspace configs: 1) Use explicit package paths for packages-oss/* to avoid matching root-level .md files. 2) Add all internal packages to vitest-config aliases including @snapback/engine. 3) For ESM-only packages, add server.deps.inline pattern. 4) Exclude non-package files like vercel-entry.ts from projects glob.

Use hybrid SDD+TDD+Constraints approach: 1) Specification provides strategic context (WHY/WHAT), 2) TDD provides tactical validation (regression prevention), 3) Constraints provide guardrails (design erosion prevention). Pure TDD insufficient for complex features - add spec layer in 3_planning.md Step 0.

Follow strict phase progression: Phase 1 (core implementation) → Phase 2 (intelligence facade) → Phase 3 (UI integration). Each phase must be complete and tested before advancing. Use `start_task()` pre-flight check at each phase boundary to verify patterns and constraints.

trigger: vitals VS Code integration, AutoDecisionIntegration wiring
action: Import WorkspaceVitals from @snapback/intelligence/vitals. Initialize in constructor with workspaceId from WorkspaceContextManager. Feed events via vitals.onFileChange() in event handler. Use getThresholdMultiplier() to adjust risk score. Call vitals.onSnapshot() after snapshot creation to release pressure. Expose getVitals() method for testing/status display.

Create vitals MCP tools in apps/mcp-server/src/tools/vitals-tools.ts. Export vitalsToolDefinitions array and handler functions. Add tool definitions to the tools array via spread (...vitalsToolDefinitions). Add handlers in CallToolRequestSchema handler with if (name === 'snapback.tool_name') pattern. Use WorkspaceVitals.for(workspaceId) for singleton access.

After modifying test files, ALWAYS grep for placeholder assertions: `grep -r 'expect(true).toBe(true)\|TODO' test/`. Treat any matches as blocking issues that must be resolved before marking task complete.
Create spec files in apps/cli/resources/cli_implementation/ with 00-overview.md as index and numbered specs (01-04) for each library integration

TODO comments indicate incomplete work and must be resolved before commit. If the work is blocked, move to __deferred__/ directory with documented unblock criteria. Lefthook pre-push hook warns on TODO markers in test files.

Every test file must cover 4 paths: happy (success), sad (expected failure), edge (boundaries/null/empty), error (unexpected failures/recovery). Use ai_dev_utils/templates/test-file.template.ts as reference. ValidationPipeline checks for this via TestLayer.
Placeholder assertions like expect(true).toBe(true) are caught by Lefthook pre-push hook. They block commits and must be replaced with real assertions testing actual behavior.

Use packages/intelligence/src/vitals/learning/ for user behavior learning (UserBehaviorLearner), per-workspace threshold calibration (ThresholdCalibrator), and trajectory prediction (TrajectoryPredictor). WorkspaceVitals wires these components and exposes recordBehavior(), getCalibratedThresholds(), getForecast() methods. Total 139 tests cover Phase 1-4 vitals implementation.

Wire learning components by: 1) Add recordBehavior() calls where user creates snapshots (AutoDecisionIntegration.ts), 2) Expose forecast/calibration/behaviorStats in MCP tools (vitals-tools.ts), 3) Add Phase 4 methods to Intelligence facade

Create proper type definitions from @snapback/contracts and use SessionWithUser instead of (session as any) casts. Import types from Better Auth, extend with app-specific fields (role, username), and create type guards for safe narrowing.

trigger: hardcoded config TODO environment settings feature flags
action: Created centralized config system at lib/config.ts importing from validated env.ts. Added 4 NEXT_PUBLIC_FEATURE_* flags with Zod transform (string "true"/"false" → boolean). Replaced 11 hardcoded config objects across layout files. Pattern: import { config } from "@/lib/config" instead of local const config = {...}

trigger: boxen test, long content assertion, wrapped content
action: When testing boxen output with long content, count total characters instead of checking consecutive strings - boxen wraps content across lines

trigger: ROUTER.md too large, context efficiency, modular rules, path targeting
action: Refactor large ROUTER.md (1363 lines) into modular rules/ directory with path-targeted .md files. Each rule has YAML frontmatter with globs for path matching. MCP server loads only relevant rules based on files being modified. Token efficiency improved by ~86%.

trigger: integration gaps, wiring, undone integration, loose ends, auth integration, 3rd party
action: Route as INTEGRATION_AUDIT task type. Follow 2_research.md workflow: 1) Search DB/API/Auth patterns, 2) Check docs_to_review/INTEGRATION_GAPS_STATUS.md, 3) grep for TODO/FIXME/(as any), 4) Generate roadmap with prioritized tickets. Update ROUTER.md Integration Debt table with findings.

trigger: vscode mock filesystem tests fail with EISDIR or path issues
action: Use path.join in Uri.joinPath mock to normalize '..' segments; delegate mockVscodeWorkspace.fs methods to Node's fs/promises for real filesystem operations

When one package needs to import from another for testing, add exports field to source package's package.json. For integration tests, prefer relative imports from source files (../../../cli/src/...) over package imports to avoid build dependency issues.

trigger: tests verify file contents but vscode.workspace.fs is mocked to return empty
action: Option A: Update setup.ts fs mocks to delegate to real Node fs operations (readFile, writeFile, stat, etc.) for true integration testing

File watching via chokidar is NOT an anti-pattern - industry standard used by 30M+ repos. Key best practices: ignoreInitial:true, awaitWriteFinish for atomic writes, ignored patterns for node_modules/.git, opt-in daemon with pause capability. PM2/nodemon pattern: separate dev watcher from production event-driven learning.

CLI watch daemon pattern: Use chokidar with ignoreInitial:true, awaitWriteFinish for atomic writes, and .on('ready') to confirm startup. Emit events for change/add/unlink, buffer signals for batch writes every 30s. Track change frequency per file to detect patterns (5x = suggest protection, 10x = generate learning). Use EventEmitter for watcher class to enable flexible event handling.

trigger: postbuild script failing DTS patch tsup
action: When postbuild DTS patch scripts fail with "could not find export statement", first verify if the exports already exist in generated output (tsup improvements may have fixed original issue). Update patch scripts to check if patching is still needed before failing: 1) Verify file exists 2) Check if target exports present 3) Exit 0 if already correct 4) Only patch if legacy pattern found. Prevents false failures when tooling improves.

tsup tree-shaking warnings like 'X is imported but never used in dist/Y.js' are informational, not errors. Common cases: 1) Re-exported types/values for API compatibility (e.g., export { LogLevel } for consumers) 2) Cross-module imports where usage is in different bundle entry point. Verify actual source usage with grep before removing - if used anywhere in src/, the warning is false positive from bundle analysis.

trigger: mcp tool naming OR tool discoverability OR LLM tool selection
action: Use action verbs (assess, validate, fetch) not passive (check, analyze). Add MCP annotations (title, readOnlyHint, destructiveHint). Include outputSchema with ranked next_actions for tool chaining. Unify namespace under snapback.* with migration layer for backward compatibility.

When merging duplicate hooks with overlapping functionality: 1) Read both implementations completely 2) Identify feature gaps (one has velocity tracking, other has section tracking + scroll progress) 3) Create unified version combining all features 4) Use proper TypeScript types (ScrollState interface) 5) Delete .tsx version, keep .ts 6) Update imports (only 1 usage found, update directly). Merged 148-line + 140-line hooks → 230-line normalized hook with full feature set. Key: Don't lose features when consolidating.

When consolidating duplicate middleware: 1) Grep for imports before deletion 2) Check if files are only referenced in docs (safe to delete) 3) Update imports to canonical location before deleting stubs 4) Legacy middleware/ directory was completely unused - all active code imports from src/middleware/

trigger: importing logger in client-side React code
action: NEVER import @snapback/infrastructure logger in files with "use client" directive. The logger uses pino which depends on Node.js-only modules (thread-stream, fs, worker_threads). For client components: use console.* methods. For server components/API routes: use logger from @snapback/infrastructure. Pattern: if file has "use client" → console.*, else → logger.*

Use glob patterns with double-star (**/) in EXCLUDE_PATTERNS to exclude nested node_modules inside test fixtures. Pattern 'node_modules' only matches top-level, use '**/node_modules/**' and '**/fixtures/**/node_modules/**' for comprehensive exclusion.

@next/bundle-analyzer requires Webpack (--no-turbo flag in Next.js 15+). For Turbopack (default), use Next.js 16.1+ experimental analyzer: `npx next experimental-analyze`. This opens an interactive UI on port 4000 to inspect production bundles, trace imports, and identify large modules.

When upgrading Next.js major versions: 1) Update core packages in pnpm catalog: next, @next/mdx, @next/third-parties, @next/bundle-analyzer to same version 2) Use catalog: references in package.json for consistency 3) Check for abandoned packages (next-contentlayer → contentlayer2 fork) 4) Peer dependency warnings for unused packages are safe to ignore
Convert __mocks__/vscode.js to .mjs with ESM imports. Use `import { vi } from 'vitest'` and `export default` instead of `require('vitest')` and `module.exports`

trigger: fumadocs configuration OR MDX documentation framework
action: Fumadocs REPLACES @next/mdx (not duplicative). Benefits: auto-generated type-safe collections, built-in search, UI components (nav/TOC), full Turbopack support. Configure via source.config.ts with collections, remark/rehype plugins. Use createMDX() wrapper in next.config.mjs. Version alignment: keep fumadocs-core, fumadocs-ui, fumadocs-mdx at compatible versions (ui/core at v15+, mdx at v14+).

trigger: CLI intelligence integration, learning loop CLI, codebase mcp to CLI
action: Use @snapback/intelligence package with different rootDir configs: Internal MCP uses ai_dev_utils/, Customer CLI uses .snapback/. Create intelligence-service.ts with singleton per workspace. New commands: snap context (pre-work), snap validate (7-layer pipeline), snap stats (learning stats). Enhance existing: patterns report (auto-promotion at 3x), patterns analyze (ValidationPipeline). See CLI-UX-005 spec.

trigger: mcp tool naming OR tool discoverability OR LLM tool selection
action: Use action verbs (assess, validate, fetch) not passive (check, analyze). Add MCP annotations (title, readOnlyHint, destructiveHint). Include outputSchema with ranked next_actions for tool chaining. Unify namespace under snapback.* with migration layer for backward compatibility. Return errors with isError:true per MCP spec.
ALWAYS run pnpm build or pnpm tsc --noEmit before declaring any TypeScript implementation complete. Never celebrate until build passes.
Set polar angle limits to Math.PI/4 (45°) to Math.PI*3/4 (135°) to constrain vertical rotation and prevent full spherical orbit

trigger: 3D model hover rotation, PresentationControls hover behavior
action: Use custom useDragRotate hook instead of PresentationControls or OrbitControls. PresentationControls responds to pointer movement (hover) by design. Create a custom hook that only responds to pointerdown (click) events with snap-back behavior after idle timeout.

trigger: React Three Fiber canvas blank on scroll
action: Add resize={{ scroll: true }} prop to Canvas component. This ensures proper rendering when scrolling by handling resize events during scroll.

trigger: 3D texture mirrored text, backwards text on screen
action: Use flipTextureHorizontal: true and/or flipTextureVertical: true in applySplitGeometry config. Different device models may need different flip combinations depending on how the GLTF model UV coordinates are set up.

Custom click-drag rotation hook location: src/components/3d/hooks/useDragRotate.ts. Features: only responds to click+drag (not hover), spring physics snap-back after idle timeout, configurable polar/azimuth limits, sensitivity control. Use with groupRef attached to a THREE.Group wrapping the model.

PresentationControls inherently responds to pointer movement (hover), not just clicks. Even with global={false}, it still tracks pointer position. For click-drag-only behavior, use custom useDragRotate hook instead.

trigger: toBeNull assertion in React component tests
action: toBeNull() is acceptable for testing components that conditionally render null - it's a specific assertion for nullity checks, not vague like toBeTruthy()

trigger: 3D model rotation fix, model facing wrong direction
action: Add Math.PI (180°) to initial Y rotation in useDragRotate options. Most GLTF device models (phones, desktops) face -Z by default and need 180° Y rotation to show front. Check deviceConfig.rotation first, otherwise use [0, Math.PI, 0] as default.

trigger: smooth rotation animation, jerky motion, discrete jumps
action: Use exponential easing (GSAP-style) with continuous interpolation: current = current + (target - current) * easingSpeed. Apply every frame for smooth motion. Default easingSpeed=0.08 for natural feel, increase to 0.1 for snappier response.

trigger: ContactShadows alignment, shadow offset, floating model
action: Use consistent ContactShadows props across all device scenes: opacity=0.35, blur=1.5, far=2, resolution=256. Adjust shadow position Y based on model ground plane (typically -1.0 to -1.5). Scale should match model footprint (4-5 for phones/tablets, 5-6 for desktops).

trigger: React Three Fiber excessive re-rendering console flood
action: Remove console.log from component render bodies. R3F components re-render at 60fps with frameloop="always". Only use console.log inside useEffect hooks, not in render functions. Pattern: console.log in PhoneModel/DesktopModel render → 60fps console flood.

When removing unused dependencies from pnpm workspace: 1) Remove from individual package.json files first 2) Remove from pnpm-workspace.yaml catalog 3) Remove from root package.json 4) Run `pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile` to update lockfile 5) Verify with `pnpm type-check` across affected packages. Dependencies tracked in 3 locations: package deps, catalog, and root.

trigger: rule registration, sorted collection, priority queue
action: Use binary search insertion instead of sort-on-every-add for O(log n) performance. Pattern: maintain sorted invariant during insertion rather than re-sorting entire collection

Extract hardcoded thresholds to named constants at file scope with JSDoc. Use const objects with 'as const' for enum-like threshold sets (e.g., FRAGILITY_THRESHOLDS = { HIGH: 0.7, MODERATE: 0.5 } as const)
Validate config parameters in constructor before assignment. Throw descriptive errors for invalid values (negative numbers, out of range, etc). Pattern: validate → assign → initialize
Use regex patterns for file type detection instead of string methods. Pattern: /\.(test|spec)\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$/ is more robust than .endsWith('.test.ts'). Extract to helper functions for reusability

After code review identifying minor improvements: TodoWrite for tracking → implement systematically → verify each change (type-check + tests) → check_patterns before commit. Run full verification suite even for small changes

Link TODOs to issue tracker IDs (INT-XXX) and label with phase scope (e.g., 'Phase 2 Enhancement'). Format: '// Phase 2 Enhancement (INT-010): Description\n// TODO: Implementation details'. Makes future work trackable

trigger: ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND after turbo clean or individual package build
action: After turbo clean or individual package builds, ALWAYS run `pnpm build` from root to apply fix-all-esm-imports.mjs globally. Individual package builds (e.g., `pnpm --filter @snapback/contracts build`) don't run the global ESM fix script.

trigger: failed to initialize MCP client - stderr output corrupts JSON-RPC
action: MCP servers must be SILENT on stderr during normal operation. Any console.log/console.error/logger output corrupts the JSON-RPC stdio protocol. Add MCP_QUIET=1 env var check to ALL packages that may output during MCP server initialization. Packages fixed: @snapback/config, @snapback/contracts, @snapback/auth, apps/mcp-server/src/config.ts

trigger: MCP server fails with StorageError or log output corrupts protocol
action: Rebuild ALL packages after modifying MCP_QUIET logging fixes: 1) pnpm --filter @snapback/infrastructure build, 2) pnpm --filter @snapback/config build, 3) pnpm --filter @snapback/contracts build, 4) node scripts/build-utils/fix-all-esm-imports.mjs, 5) pnpm --filter @snapback/mcp-server build. Changes to packages/infrastructure/src/logging/logger.ts won't take effect until dist/ is regenerated.
